name: Keyhive
description: Local-first access control library providing convergent capabilities (concap), group management CRDTs, and end-to-end encryption with causal key management (BeeKEM) for CRDT-based collaborative applications. Designed as a foundational authorization layer beneath identity — enables secure collaboration with read/write/admin roles, coordination-free revocation, and trust-minimized sync servers without requiring a central authority. Targets Automerge ecosystem but designed to be general-purpose for local-first applications .
owner: Ink & Switch (independent research lab); Keyhive team: Alex Good, John Mumm, Brooklyn Zelenka (project lead)

entityAttributes.authenticationIdentity: Deliberately excludes user identity — provides cryptographic authorization beneath identity layer. All entities identified by Ed25519 public keys (documents, users, groups, devices). Self-certifying delegation chains. Identity binding (name registration, user verification) explicitly left to layers above Keyhive
entityAttributes.storageModel: Data encrypted-at-rest with causal key management; encryption boundaries based on Automerge Binary Format compressed chunks; symmetric keys for each chunk with causal predecessor key inclusion; Sedimentree for probabilistic compression and encryption of CRDT updates
entityAttributes.interoperability: Automerge ecosystem (primary target); designed to be general-purpose for any local-first application; beelay-core provides auth-enabled sync protocol; Automerge Discord community; planned integration with broader local-first ecosystem
entityAttributes.dataPortability: Full portability (user controls keypairs; authorization state travels with data in CRDT; no central server dependency; capability chains are self-certifying and portable)
entityAttributes.governanceDecisionMaking: Ink & Switch research lab (independent, non-commercial research lab; open-source community contributions via GitHub; Code of Conduct; Contributing guidelines)
entityAttributes.identityStandards: Not applicable in traditional sense — Keyhive deliberately operates below identity standards. Uses Ed25519/X25519 cryptographic keys as identifiers; convergent capabilities (concap) as novel access control primitive between object-capabilities and certificate-capabilities (UCAN, zcap-ld)
entityAttributes.didMethodsSupported: None (deliberately excludes identity layer; entities identified by raw Ed25519 public keys rather than DIDs; DID binding left to layers above)
entityAttributes.keyManagement: User-controlled Ed25519/X25519 keypairs per device; BeeKEM (novel Continuous Group Key Agreement protocol) for group key management with logarithmic performance; Diffie-Hellman key exchange at tree nodes; BLAKE3 key derivation function for ratcheting; periodic key rotation for post-compromise security; causal key chains for encrypted chunk access
entityAttributes.credentialTypes: Convergent capabilities (concap): stateful, CRDT-based capability tokens that sit between object-capabilities and certificate-capabilities; delegation chains with read/write/admin/pull access effects; group membership as implicit credential
entityAttributes.verificationMethod: Cryptographic signature verification (Ed25519); self-certifying delegation chains (each capability cryptographically proves authorization path); ECDH shared secrets for encryption/decryption; no external verification service required
entityAttributes.privacyFeatures: End-to-end encryption (data encrypted-at-rest; sync servers see only random-looking bytes); trust-minimized sync servers (cannot read plaintext); pull access control (restricts even retrieval of ciphertext to authorized parties); post-compromise security via BeeKEM key rotation; forward secrecy sacrificed for CRDT compatibility (requires full causal history) but concurrent/future chunks remain secret
entityAttributes.authenticationMethods: Cryptographic signatures (Ed25519); capability delegation chains (self-certifying); no passwords, no usernames, no external auth providers — purely cryptographic
entityAttributes.revocationMechanism: Coordination-free revocation via convergent capabilities; BeeKEM tree blanking (revoked member's leaf and path blanked; concurrent operations by revoked members guaranteed not to survive); group CRDT handles concurrent add/remove conflicts deterministically
entityAttributes.agentTypesSupported: Public keys representing: documents, users (individuals), groups (teams/organizations), devices (user device management behind proxy identities); designed to be extensible to any entity representable by a public key
entityAttributes.walletClientTypes: SDK/Library integration (Rust crate keyhive_core; WASM package keyhive_wasm with TypeScript bindings; beelay-core for sync); no standalone wallet — embeds into applications
entityAttributes.recoveryMechanisms: Not yet specified (pre-alpha; key loss recovery mechanisms not documented; device management via group delegation provides some redundancy — multiple devices behind a user proxy)
entityAttributes.complianceRegulations: Not applicable at current stage (pre-alpha research project; explicitly excludes FIPS compliance as antigoal; no security audit performed; privacy-preserving by design via E2EE)
entityAttributes.credentialExchangeProtocols: Convergent capability delegation (signed delegation graphs traversed locally; no external exchange protocol — capabilities travel with CRDT data via sync); beelay protocol for auth-enabled sync
entityAttributes.trustFramework: Cryptographic capabilities only (no central authority; trust rooted in Ed25519 key ownership and delegation chain verification; trust-minimized sync servers; designed for environments ranging from 'planning a surprise party' to 'journalists and activists')
entityAttributes.costModel: Free / open source (Apache 2.0; research project; no commercial offering)
entityAttributes.censorshipResistance: High (no central authority; E2EE data; trust-minimized sync servers interchangeable; authorization travels with data; designed for peer-to-peer and offline operation under arbitrary network partition)
entityAttributes.developmentTools: Rust workspace (cargo/rustup) — three crates: keyhive_core (core signing, encryption, delegation; Ed25519/X25519 keypairs, BeeKEM CGKA, capability chains, Sedimentree sync), beelay-core (auth-enabled sync protocol over E2E-encrypted data; RPC over HTTPS/WebSocket/TLS), keyhive_wasm (WASM wrapper around keyhive_core with TypeScript bindings via wasm-bindgen/wasm-pack); Nix flake for reproducible cross-platform development environment; GitHub Actions CI for native and WASM test pipelines. Integration target: Automerge CRDT documents (co-developed Beelay sync protocol). Pre-alpha; no security audit at time of writing; not recommended for production. Repository: github.com/inkandswitch/keyhive.
entityAttributes.permissions: Apache 2.0 (open source) — keyhive, keyhive_core, keyhive_wasm, and beelay-core are all published under the Apache 2.0 license (permissive, patent grant included, commercial use allowed). Internal access control model uses CCAP (Convergent Capabilities), a capability-based delegation system where permissions are cryptographically bound and merge conflict-free — this is the runtime authorization model, not the software license.

name: Affinidi
description: A Temasek-founded (2020) data and identity management company building open-standard-based developer infrastructure for consent-driven, privacy-preserving credential issuance, verification, and data sharing. Products include the Affinidi Trust Development Kit (TDK, open source), Affinidi Vault (personal data store), and Affinidi Iota Framework (consent-based selective data sharing via OID4VP). Active pilots include Singapore-India cross-border employment verification (Dec 2025) and cross-border trade interoperability with IMDA. ⚠️ Governance flag: entirely Temasek-controlled (sovereign wealth fund); not community-governed.
owner: Affinidi Pte Ltd — founded by Temasek (Singapore sovereign wealth fund); 4 of 5 board members Temasek-affiliated; CEO Glenn Gore (ex-AWS)
license: Closed source / Partially open / Open source

entityAttributes.authenticationIdentity: W3C DID-based identity; Affinidi Trust Network (ATN) — trust registry for ecosystem participants; Affinidi Vault as user identity hub; consent-first data sharing model; credentials cryptographically signed and verifiable without Affinidi intermediation
entityAttributes.storageModel: Centralized cloud vault (Affinidi-hosted, user-encrypted); credentials stored in Affinidi Vault; VCs portable to external wallets via standard protocols
entityAttributes.interoperability: High standards alignment — W3C VC/DID, OID4VP/OID4VCI, DIF PEX; collaboration with IMDA, background screening partners (Avvanz, eeCheck, RMI), GFTN ; interoperability with standard-compliant wallets
entityAttributes.dataPortability: Yes — W3C VC standards ensure credential portability; OID4VP for presentation to any compliant verifier; user can share from Vault to any OID4VP-compliant relying party
entityAttributes.governanceDecisionMaking: ⚠️ Corporate-controlled: Affinidi Pte Ltd wholly owned/controlled by Temasek; no community governance model; no open standards body stewardship; CEO and board Temasek-affiliated; decisions made by Temasek/company leadership
entityAttributes.identityStandards: W3C DID Core; W3C Verifiable Credentials Data Model; DIF Presentation Exchange; OpenID Foundation OID4VP; OpenID Foundation OID4VCI; OAuth 2.0
entityAttributes.didMethodsSupported: Multiple — platform supports standard DID methods via W3C DID Core; specific methods not enumerated in public docs (did:web likely primary)
entityAttributes.keyManagement: User-controlled keys in Affinidi Vault; cryptographic signing via W3C VC proof mechanisms; key details abstracted by platform SDK
entityAttributes.credentialTypes: Employment credentials; professional background check credentials; trade/business credentials; health credentials (COVID-era origins); educational credentials; government-issued credentials (ID, passport); custom enterprise credentials
entityAttributes.verificationMethod: Cryptographic proof verification via W3C VC standard; VP Token verification via OID4VP; tamper-evident cryptographic proof chains; no Affinidi server required for verification
entityAttributes.privacyFeatures: Selective disclosure via PEX / OID4VP — only specific data points shared per transaction; processing shifted to user Vault (not third-party servers); consent-first model; privacy-by-design philosophy; E2E encryption in Vault
entityAttributes.authenticationMethods: W3C DID-based authentication; OID4VP-based authentication flows; consent-driven data sharing as authentication signal
entityAttributes.revocationMechanism: W3C VC revocation (StatusList2021 or equivalent); specific revocation registry implementation not publicly detailed
entityAttributes.agentTypesSupported: Human users (via Vault); business entities (issuers/verifiers via platform API); AI agent compatibility mentioned in recent positioning ("enabling secure collaboration between individuals, businesses, and AI agents")
entityAttributes.walletClientTypes: Affinidi Vault (proprietary, web); standard OID4VC-compliant external wallets supported
entityAttributes.recoveryMechanisms: Platform-managed (vault recovery via Affinidi account); specific recovery primitives not publicly documented
entityAttributes.complianceRegulations: Singapore PDPA alignment; cross-border compliance (Singapore-India talent corridor pilot with IMDA); eIDAS-adjacent (European interoperability pilots implied); GDPR awareness (Berlin hub)
entityAttributes.credentialExchangeProtocols: OID4VP (OpenID for Verifiable Presentations); OID4VCI (OpenID for VC Issuance); DIF Presentation Exchange (PEX); VP Tokens
entityAttributes.trustFramework: Affinidi Trust Network (ATN) — proprietary trust registry for ecosystem participants; trust established via W3C VC cryptographic proofs; relying on Temasek ecosystem and external partner relationships
entityAttributes.costModel: Freemium developer access (Affinidi Portal); enterprise SaaS pricing for platform features; TDK open source (free); specific pricing not publicly disclosed
entityAttributes.censorshipResistance: Low — centralized Affinidi Vault and ATN; Temasek ownership creates Singapore-jurisdiction dependency; W3C VC standards theoretically enable migration but practical ecosystem lock-in via ATN
entityAttributes.developmentTools: Affinidi Trust Development Kit (TDK) — open-source TypeScript SDK for building privacy-preserving VC applications; Affinidi Portal (developer console, credential schema builder); open APIs (OID4VP/OID4VCI endpoints); starter kits and hackathon resources
entityAttributes.permissions: Open source TDK: no license restrictions; platform SaaS: enterprise terms apply; underlying standards (W3C VC/DID, OID4VP) open; Affinidi Trust Network participation governed by platform terms

name: IdentiKey
description: Decentralized identity and data sovereignty infrastructure using dCypher proxy re-encryption (recryption) technology to enable user-controlled encrypted cloud storage and sharing. Provides self-sovereign identity with persona management, a cross-account attestation-based social graph and trust/reputation network, self-sovereign single sign-on, blockchain-agnostic wallet functionality, and verifiable data publishing — all with user-custodied private keys and without trusting central intermediaries
owner: Duke Jones / Sovereign Technologies (duke@worldtree.io); IdentiKey project (identikey.io)

entityAttributes.authenticationIdentity: Self-sovereign identity with user-generated cryptographic keypairs; multiple personas per user (each persona is a signed DID with self-attested metadata that can be pseudonymous or linked to legal identity); self-sovereign single sign-on via private key authentication integrated with popular web frameworks; persona switching with protections against correlatable data leakage across personas
entityAttributes.storageModel: Encrypted cloud storage via dCypher proxy (user-controlled keys; storage provider never sees plaintext even if hacked); verifiable data registry using content-based addressing (hash-referenced, self-verifying); supports existing cloud providers (iCloud, Google Drive)
entityAttributes.interoperability: Works with existing cloud storage providers via dCypher proxy integration; blockchain-agnostic wallet functionality (per-blockchain plugins); planned integrations with popular web frameworks for Web2 self-authentication; interoperable data ecosystems with user-controlled read/write permissions to third-party applications
entityAttributes.dataPortability: Full portability (user owns encryption keys; data can move between services; content-addressed storage allows data to be stored in any compatible service)
entityAttributes.governanceDecisionMaking: Individual/project-led (Duke Jones / Sovereign Technologies)
entityAttributes.identityStandards: Self-sovereign identity (SSI) principles; W3C DID (account-published DIDs); Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) for anonymized authoritative attestation verification
entityAttributes.didMethodsSupported: Account-published DIDs (self-signed DID documents; specific DID method TBD)
entityAttributes.keyManagement: User-custodied private keys stored on-device using secure local storage and device-specific secure enclaves; wallet keys separable from account keys and attachable to different personas; MPC-based multisig without smart contracts for shared account management
entityAttributes.credentialTypes: Self-attested metadata (cleartext or encrypted with selective disclosure); cross-account attestations (directed graph between accounts); authoritative attestations (KYC-style, can be anonymized via ZKP); attestation schemas with morphisms across compatible schemas
entityAttributes.verificationMethod: Cryptographic signature verification (data signed by author keys for provenance and authenticity); content-hash self-verification for data integrity; ZKP for anonymous proof of authoritative attestations
entityAttributes.privacyFeatures: Proxy re-encryption (data remains encrypted to service providers); selective disclosure (ZKP to demonstrate proof of a value/signature without disclosing it, hierarchical tree-based public-key encryption, proxy re-encryption for access delegation); per-item encryption (sweeping data breach impossible); real-time AI agent activity feed and control panel
entityAttributes.authenticationMethods: Private key-based self-authentication (self-sovereign SSO); planned integrations with popular web frameworks for Web2 login experience
entityAttributes.revocationMechanism: User-controlled revocation of viewing passes / access permissions at any time; AI agent permissions instantly revocable via control panel
entityAttributes.agentTypesSupported: Humans (interactive, with multiple personas); AI agents (secure delegation of specific data access permissions with real-time activity monitoring); non-interactive entities (via MPC-based multisig)
entityAttributes.walletClientTypes: Identity management dashboard (web/mobile planned); blockchain-agnostic wallet with per-blockchain UX plugins; MPC-based multisig for group account management
entityAttributes.recoveryMechanisms: Federated guardian system (opt-in): user chooses n guardians; recovery key sharded across guardians; recovery requires authenticating with a subset of chosen guardians using multiple methods (username/password, SMS, TOTP, etc.); guardians are trusted entities with professional uptime guarantees; system remains firmly non-custodial
entityAttributes.complianceRegulations: Privacy-first design (zero-knowledge to service providers); d/acc compliant (decentralized, democratic, differential, defensive); reduced attack surface for PII storage; ESIGN Act compatibility for legally binding cryptographic signatures (Ricardian Contracts support)
entityAttributes.credentialExchangeProtocols: Verifiable data registry (content-addressed, hash-referenced, signed attestations published to platform-agnostic data storage layer); selective disclosure via ZKP, hierarchical encryption, and proxy re-encryption
entityAttributes.trustFramework: User sovereignty + cryptographic verification (no central authority); attestation-based directed social graph forming decentralized trust/reputation network; authoritative attestations for sybil-resistance (anonymizable via ZKP)
entityAttributes.censorshipResistance: High (user controls keys; no central authority can revoke access; data encrypted end-to-end; content-addressed storage can use any compatible service)

name: First Person Project / Network
description: Decentralized proof of personhood infrastructure using Personhood Credentials (PHCs) and Verifiable Relationship Credentials (VRCs) to prove real unique humans with authentic first-person trust relationships, forming a privacy-preserving decentralized trust graph. No centralized database — all credentials stored in user digital wallets. Sparked by the August 2024 Personhood Credentials paper in response to generative AI's ability to impersonate humans. First real-world pilot: Linux Kernel project (kernel.org) to prevent supply chain attacks like the Jia Tan/XZ Utils incident
owner: First Person Project (multi-stakeholder collaboration led by Drummond Reed); collaborating LF projects: LF Decentralized Trust (LFDT), Trust Over IP (ToIP), Decentralized Identity Foundation (DIF), and OpenWallet Foundation (OWF); additional partners: Ayra Association, Customer Commons; planned: First Person Network Cooperative (FPNC) as governing digital cooperative
stack: Implementation-dependent four-layer stack modeled after TCP/IP
Trust Spanning Protocol as keystone protocol
zero-knowledge proof cryptography architecture being developed by LFDT CTO Hart Montgomery

entityAttributes.authenticationIdentity: Decentralized Identity (DIDs) with two new credential types: Personhood Credentials (PHCs — issued by any ecosystem that can attest holder is a real unique person within that ecosystem, e.g., governments, employers, universities, nonprofits) and Verifiable Relationship Credentials (VRCs — issued peer-to-peer between PHC holders to attest to verifiable first-person trust relationships, like digital business cards). Threshold-based: progressive accumulation of PHCs and VRCs makes impersonation increasingly difficult
entityAttributes.storageModel: User wallet only (decentralized; each member controls their own portable subgraph of trust relationships in their own digital agents and wallets); FPNC sovereign cloud services planned for convenient messaging and data exchange without third-party surveillance
entityAttributes.interoperability: High: any W3C VC-compatible wallet; ToIP-compatible systems; standardized via Decentralized Trust Graph Working Group (DTGWG) as joint ToIP-DIF participation Working Group; interoperability across all five layers of credential lifecycle (identifiers, credentials, wallets/agents, ecosystems, trust networks)
entityAttributes.dataPortability: Full portability (standard W3C VCs; wallet-to-wallet; no platform lock-in; credentials portable across all compatible ecosystems)
entityAttributes.governanceDecisionMaking: Multi-stakeholder consortium (LFDT, ToIP, DIF, OWF, Ayra Association, Customer Commons) → planned First Person Network Cooperative (FPNC): digital cooperative established for exclusive purpose of developing, growing, and maintaining First Person Network as digital public utility; any individual or trust community who joins is qualified to join FPNC; FPNC establishes governance framework policies
entityAttributes.identityStandards: W3C DID; W3C Verifiable Credentials; Trust Over IP (ToIP) four-layer stack; Trust Spanning Protocol (TSP); Personhood Credentials (PHCs); Verifiable Relationship Credentials (VRCs); zero-knowledge proof standards
entityAttributes.didMethodsSupported: Multiple methods to be supported (implementation-dependent; uses cryptographically verifiable identifiers compatible with zero-knowledge cryptography; specific DID methods being specified by DTGWG)
entityAttributes.keyManagement: User-controlled in digital wallets (implementation-dependent; FPNC offers digital wallet backup/recovery services to cooperative members)
entityAttributes.credentialTypes: Personhood Credentials (PHCs — ecosystem-issued, attesting holder is real unique person in that ecosystem); Verifiable Relationship Credentials (VRCs — peer-to-peer, attesting verified first-person trust relationships); threshold-based presentation (show number of relationships exceeding certain thresholds without revealing specific relationships, similar to LinkedIn '500+ connections')
entityAttributes.verificationMethod: Zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs): can verify holder is a unique human with a certain threshold of trust relationships without learning anything more; cryptographic signature verification on credentials; no personal data shared during verification
entityAttributes.privacyFeatures: Zero-knowledge proofs for all verification; selective disclosure; no global biometric database; no centralized credential database; local biometrics only (if used); pseudonymous threshold presentation (reveals relationship counts as thresholds, not specific relationships); no tracking or surveillance
entityAttributes.authenticationMethods: Cryptographic signatures on credentials; ZKP-based proof presentation; Trust Spanning Protocol for secure credential exchange; biometric verification is issuer-side only and local (not global database)
entityAttributes.revocationMechanism: To be specified by DTGWG (likely standard VC revocation mechanisms; credentials are bilateral — either party can terminate a VRC relationship)
entityAttributes.agentTypesSupported: Humans (personhood verification); Personal AI agents (First Person AI agents offered through FPNC — act exclusively in user's interest with no vendor conflict of interest; secure delegation via credentials); trust communities (organizational entities as PHC issuers)
entityAttributes.walletClientTypes: Any W3C VC-compatible digital wallet (Apple, Google, Samsung smartphone wallets; dedicated third-party wallets; FPNC cooperative wallet with backup/recovery)
entityAttributes.recoveryMechanisms: FPNC services include digital wallet backup/recovery for cooperative members; otherwise wallet-dependent
entityAttributes.complianceRegulations: Privacy-preserving by design; supports Permissionless Entry (anyone can join without central approval) and Worldwide Participation (works across all jurisdictions); no global biometric database; credentials meet W3C standards for international interoperability
entityAttributes.credentialExchangeProtocols: Trust Spanning Protocol (TSP) for secure credential exchange; DIDComm (likely); W3C VC presentation/verification protocols; zero-knowledge proof presentation
entityAttributes.trustFramework: Decentralized trust graph: collection of all First Person credential issuers and holders forms an interconnected web of verifiable first-person trust relationships; no central authority; trust is grounded in real-world relationships captured as cryptographic credentials; progressive trust strengthening through accumulating PHCs and VRCs from diverse ecosystems
entityAttributes.costModel: Free to users (FPNC cooperative offers services at lowest cost to members; ecosystem fund for network sustainability)
entityAttributes.censorshipResistance: High (no central authority; decentralized like the Internet; credentials in user wallets; no centralized database to attack or censor; FPNC sovereign cloud services operated cooperatively without third-party terms of service)

name: GreenCheck
description: A proof-of-personhood and web-of-trust identity system using three-friend authentication and intersectional identity to enable high-trust networks. Each human gets one and only one GreenCheck ID (GCID), forming nodes in a trust graph where validated relationships form edges. Designed as infrastructure for Networked Adaptive Organisms (NAOs) — trust-network-based DAOs for emergent coordination .
owner: NooNAO / NooNet (Brad DeGraf and Charles Blass); NooNet is a fiscally-sponsored project of Planetwork, a 501(c)(3); development by InsightNetwork (kfancy on GitHub)
stack: ArangoDB graph database for the trust graph backend

entityAttributes.authenticationIdentity: Intersectional identity + three-friend authentication: users prove sole control of multiple attributes (email, phone via 2FA, OAuth with social services, bookmarklet login tests); uniqueness attested via web-of-trust validation by people who know the user, to a stated confidence level (e.g., 98%)
entityAttributes.storageModel: Centralized server with federation path: GreenCheck server stores claims and issues authentication tokens; design specifies that all nodes/edges for a user can be encapsulated in a cryptographically secure microledger portable to any application independent of GreenCheck; IPFS via Ceramic/Hypercore discussed for microledger hosting
entityAttributes.interoperability: SSO to any supporting application via GreenCheck IDs; microledger encapsulation allows any application to edit/maintain user identity data independently; blockchain-independent but Web3-friendly (designed for 'Web 2.5' via MagicLink-style onboarding); integration with NextGraph planned (gc-claims-link built for NextGraph compatibility)
entityAttributes.dataPortability: High (by design): user's complete identity graph (all nodes and edges) can be encapsulated in a portable cryptographic microledger usable by any application; multiple DIDs can be 'owned' by a single GC DID
entityAttributes.governanceDecisionMaking: Fractal network cooperative with liquid democracy: intended to be structured as a self-owning, self-governing social graph; 'extreme decisioning' via trust network delegation/proxy links weighted by domain-specific authoritativeness (network centrality); based on Smartocracy whitepaper (2005 LANL) predating liquid democracy by five years
entityAttributes.identityStandards: W3C DIDs (user can have multiple DIDs owned by their GC DID); Verifiable Credentials; JLINC microledger specification; OAuth (for social service attribute proofs)
entityAttributes.didMethodsSupported: JLINC-based DIDs (microledger-backed); design supports multiple DIDs per user owned by a primary GC DID
entityAttributes.keyManagement: Cryptographic attestations for ownership claims; authentication tokens issued by GreenCheck server upon 2FA verification; mobile device keystore (SecureStore) for token storage in native contexts; web localStorage for browser contexts
entityAttributes.credentialTypes: Proof of personhood (attestation of uniqueness — 'this DID corresponds to one and only one human in the GC registry'); ownership attestations (cryptographically secure verifiable claims to control of attributes like email, phone, social accounts); peer validations (other users validate ownership claims, creating trust graph edges)
entityAttributes.verificationMethod: Web of trust / three-friend authentication: validators who personally know the user attest to their claims; validations include the GCIDs of validators; attestations carry a stated confidence level; graph analytics detect anomalies for sybil resistance
entityAttributes.privacyFeatures: Pseudonymity (multiple DIDs owned by one GC DID); zero-knowledge attestations (can return only verification that a DID corresponds to a unique human without revealing identity); scoped information disclosure (user controls what additional information is shared beyond uniqueness proof)
entityAttributes.authenticationMethods: Phone 2FA (primary: phone number collected, GreenCheck server sends 2FA token, user verifies token to receive authentication token); OAuth roundtrips with social services; email 2FA; bookmarklet/browser extension for login-at-application proofs
entityAttributes.revocationMechanism: TBD (not specified in available documentation; microledger versioning could support revocation via updated entries)
entityAttributes.agentTypesSupported: Humans only (explicitly designed for proof of personhood — 'any human can get one and only one GreenCheck ID'; no organizational or machine agent support documented)
entityAttributes.walletClientTypes: Web application (greencheck.world); mobile-compatible library (gc-claims-link with SecureStore support); intended for embedding in host applications (library designed for NextGraph integration)
entityAttributes.recoveryMechanisms: Three-friend authentication (implicit: the web-of-trust validators who attested to the user's identity could re-validate after recovery; microledger portability means identity persists across custodians)
entityAttributes.complianceRegulations: TBD (no specific regulatory compliance documented; designed for sufficient decentralization per Varun Srinivasan's framework)
entityAttributes.credentialExchangeProtocols: Custom (GreenCheck server API with basic authentication headers; claims retrieval via authenticated token; microledger exchange for portable identity)
entityAttributes.trustFramework: Web of trust with economic incentives: validations have a cost/value that incentivizes high trust density; sybil and collusion resistance through cost barriers and detection mechanisms; trust density measured via graph analytics; chain of accountability through invitation-only growth (every participant has an accountability chain to their inviter)
entityAttributes.costModel: Free for users (nonprofit infrastructure); validations have implicit cost/value to incentivize quality; future plans for collective value capture and emergent allocation as a fractal commons
entityAttributes.censorshipResistance: Moderate (by design): sufficiently decentralized — users own direct relationship with their audience and developers can always build on the network; microledger portability means no single custodian can revoke identity; federation of custodians planned but not yet implemented

name: ERC-4337
description: Account abstraction standard implementing smart contract wallets as primary user accounts via a parallel UserOperation mempool and singleton EntryPoint contract, requiring no Ethereum consensus-layer changes.
owner: Ethereum Community / Ethereum Foundation contributors

entityAttributes.permissions: Permissionless; any address can deploy an ERC-4337 compatible account; bundlers and paymasters operate in open market
entityAttributes.developmentTools: Alchemy Account Kit, Pimlico bundler, Biconomy, ZeroDev SDK, Stackup; OpenZeppelin Account Abstraction library; eth-infinitism reference implementation
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Final; EntryPoint contract audited by OpenZeppelin and deployed at canonical address on all major EVM chains; successor ERC-7702 (Pectra upgrade) introduced at protocol level
entityAttributes.trustFramework: Bundler reputation staking; paymaster staking via EntryPoint StakeManager; on-chain EntryPoint contract as trust root; custom account validation logic (ECDSA, multisig, passkey, social recovery)
entityAttributes.targetApplicationDomain: Consumer wallet UX, gasless onboarding, social recovery, DAO/multisig accounts, session keys, subscription payments
entityAttributes.deploymentModel: Singleton EntryPoint per chain; per-user smart account contracts; paymaster contracts optionally deployed per application
entityAttributes.ecosystem: Ethereum (and all EVM-compatible chains via canonical EntryPoint address)

name: ERC-725

owner: Fabian Vogelsteller / Ethereum Community

entityAttributes.permissions: Permissionless deployment; key-based execution rights; operator/controller patterns
entityAttributes.developmentTools: erc725.js library, LUKSO tools, OpenZeppelin adaptations, ERC725Alliance reference implementations
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Final; foundational to LUKSO blockchain and Universal Profiles; adopted in ERC-725Y form across identity-focused projects
entityAttributes.trustFramework: Key manager contract with role-based execution rights; claim verifier contracts for on-chain attestations; controller key hierarchy
entityAttributes.targetApplicationDomain: Decentralized identity, self-sovereign identity (SSI), Universal Profiles, DAO membership, credential management, on-chain reputation
entityAttributes.deploymentModel: Per-identity contract deployment; each user or entity has a dedicated ERC-725 contract instance
entityAttributes.ecosystem: Ethereum; particularly adopted in LUKSO ecosystem

name: EIP-4361 (SIWE)
description: Sign-In with Ethereum standard enabling Ethereum accounts to authenticate with off-chain services via a structured, human-readable signed message, replacing OAuth/social login with user-controlled cryptographic identity.
owner: SpruceID (Spruce Systems, Inc.)

entityAttributes.permissions: Permissionless; any dapp or service may implement SIWE; no central authority
entityAttributes.developmentTools: siwe npm package (TypeScript), siwe-rs (Rust), siwe-go (Go), siwe-py (Python); MetaMask native integration via MIP-0003; WalletConnect support; Auth0 SIWE plugin
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Final EIP; 3M+ library downloads; integrated into MetaMask, WalletConnect, Auth0; widely adopted in Web3 authentication flows

entityAttributes.ecosystem: Ethereum (chain-agnostic message format; chain-id field enables multi-chain adaptation)

entityAttributes.targetApplicationDomain: AI agent infrastructure, autonomous agent coordination, multi-agent systems, agentic DeFi, AI-powered DAO tooling
entityAttributes.deploymentModel: Three singleton registry contracts on Ethereum mainnet; agents instantiate identity tokens from the Identity registry

name: CosmWasm

entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Production-grade; used in 30+ Cosmos app chains including Neutron, Osmosis, and Juno; IBC-integrated; ongoing active development

name: ink!
description: Rust-based embedded domain-specific language (eDSL) for writing WebAssembly smart contracts on Substrate/Polkadot chains, offering Rust's type safety and tooling for the pallet-contracts environment.
owner: Originally Parity Technologies; transferred to ink! Alliance (use-ink organization); discontinued January 2026 due to lack of funding

entityAttributes.permissions: Permissioned by parachain governance; open deployment on parachains that enable pallet-contracts; deposit required per contract
entityAttributes.developmentTools: cargo-contract (build, test, deploy CLI); drink! (lightweight local testing); DRink! (TUI-based chainless dev tool); rust-analyzer and Clippy (native Rust tooling); Contracts UI (browser-based deployment)
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Mature for its ecosystem; reached ink! 5.x before discontinuation; never achieved broad adoption comparable to Solidity or CosmWasm
entityAttributes.trustFramework: Substrate runtime-level trust; contract owner patterns; no built-in reputation layer; pallet-contracts enforces storage deposit as spam prevention
entityAttributes.targetApplicationDomain: DeFi on Polkadot parachains, cross-parachain dApps, gaming, NFTs (PSP-34 standard), DAO tooling — all now in question following discontinuation

name: NEAR Contract Standards
description: Suite of Rust-based smart contract standards for the NEAR Protocol, including NEP-141 (fungible tokens), NEP-171 (NFTs), NEP-145 (storage management), and NEP-297 (events), providing interoperable primitives for the NEAR ecosystem.

entityAttributes.developmentTools: near-sdk-rs (Rust SDK), near-sdk-js (JS SDK), NEAR CLI, near-workspaces (integration testing), Pagoda / NEAR developer console
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: NEP-141 and NEP-171 are ratified standards; broadly adopted by NEAR ecosystem wallets, DEXes, and marketplaces (Ref Finance, Paras, etc.)
entityAttributes.trustFramework: NEAR's account-based model: contracts are named accounts; cross-contract calls are async with callbacks; no built-in reputation layer; storage staking as economic spam prevention
entityAttributes.targetApplicationDomain: DeFi, NFTs, gaming, DAOs, social apps (NEAR Social / SocialDB), civic coordination, identity (linkdrop pattern)
entityAttributes.deploymentModel: Per-account contract deployment; each NEAR account can hold exactly one contract; factory pattern used for multi-instance deployments

name: ERC-6551
description: Non-Fungible Token Bound Accounts (TBA): assigns a unique smart contract wallet (EVM account) to every ERC-721 NFT via a permissionless singleton registry and CREATE2 deterministic address derivation. Enables NFTs to own assets, accumulate on-chain history, execute transactions, and participate in governance — transforming static NFTs into composable on-chain identities.
owner: Future Primitive (Benny Giang, Jayden Windle, and contributors)
stack: ERC-1167 minimal proxy pattern for TBA deployment

entityAttributes.permissions: Permissionless: anyone may call the registry to create a TBA for any existing ERC-721 token; no whitelist or approval required
entityAttributes.developmentTools: Tokenbound SDK; OpenZeppelin Contracts; Hardhat; Foundry; Etherscan verification
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Final EIP; reference registry deployed and immutable on Ethereum mainnet; backward-compatible with all existing ERC-721 tokens
entityAttributes.trustFramework: Trustless: TBA execution authorized by NFT ownership (ownerOf); registry is a permissionless singleton contract; CREATE2 address determinism is cryptographically verifiable; no admin keys or upgrade mechanism in registry
entityAttributes.targetApplicationDomain: On-chain identity and reputation; DAO membership NFTs with embedded governance history; gaming (character NFTs owning items); loyalty and credential accumulation; DeFi (NFT-owned portfolio positions); civic participation records
entityAttributes.deploymentModel: Singleton registry deployed once per chain; individual TBA accounts deployed on-demand per NFT via registry; TBA implementations are separate contracts
entityAttributes.ecosystem: Ethereum (also deployable on all EVM-compatible chains)

name: ERC-5114
description: Soulbound Badge standard defining non-transferable tokens that are permanently bound to a specific ERC-721 NFT (persona) at the time of minting. Neither the holder nor the minter may transfer or revoke the badge after issuance. Designed for persistent, censorship-resistant credential and reputation attestation attached to on-chain identities rather than wallet addresses.
owner: Micah Zoltu
stack: requires ERC-721 for the target NFT persona
badgeUri must point to immutable content-addressable resources

entityAttributes.permissions: Permissionless minting (any contract may issue badges); post-mint transfer is unconditionally blocked; no admin revocation — immutability is a core design invariant
entityAttributes.developmentTools: OpenZeppelin Contracts (custom extension); Hardhat; Foundry; standard EVM tooling
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Review status; not yet Final; conceptual adoption is strong but competing standards (ERC-5484 Consensual SBT, ERC-4973 Account-Bound Tokens) create ecosystem fragmentation
entityAttributes.trustFramework: Immutability as trust: badge issuance is on-chain and permanent; content-addressable URIs prevent post-hoc metadata manipulation; trust derives from the issuing contract's identity and reputation rather than a central registry
entityAttributes.targetApplicationDomain: Civic participation credentials; DAO membership and contribution records; educational and professional certifications; reputation badges; attendance proofs; KYC attestations; voter eligibility records
entityAttributes.deploymentModel: Per-issuer badge contract deployed independently; badges bound to any target ERC-721 persona at mint time

name: ICS-20
description: Interchain Standard 20 (Fungible Token Transfer): the IBC application-layer protocol defining packet data structures, state machine logic, and encoding for permissionless cross-chain fungible token transfers. Implements a lock-and-mint / burn-and-unlock mechanism with IBC denomination tracing to preserve asset provenance across multi-hop transfers. The de facto cross-chain token standard for the Cosmos interchain ecosystem.
owner: Interchain Foundation / IBC Protocol team

entityAttributes.permissions: Permissionless: any IBC-enabled chain may open channels and initiate ICS-20 token transfers; no whitelisting of chains, channels, or denominations required
entityAttributes.developmentTools: ibc-go (Go SDK module); ibc-rs (Rust); Hermes relayer; Go Relayer; Interchain Test framework; CosmJS; Keplr wallet
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Production-grade standard in continuous operation since 2021; used by 100+ IBC-connected chains and averaging 2M+ monthly active users across the interchain; IBC v2 / Eureka extends ICS-20 reach to non-Cosmos chains
entityAttributes.trustFramework: Trust-minimized: security reduces to the security of participating chains and their light clients; no centralized bridge operator; cryptographic proof verification at the destination chain; Byzantine-fault-tolerant within IBC's threat model
entityAttributes.targetApplicationDomain: Cross-chain DeFi (DEX, lending, liquidity); interchain governance; cross-chain staking; multi-chain DAO treasury management; civic applications spanning multiple sovereign chains
entityAttributes.deploymentModel: Module deployed as part of Cosmos SDK chain runtime; channels established via IBC handshake per chain pair; permissionless channel creation
entityAttributes.ecosystem: Cosmos (also extending to Ethereum, Solana, and beyond via IBC v2 / Eureka)

name: SPL Token Program
description: Solana Program Library Token Program: the canonical on-chain program defining fungible and non-fungible token standards for the Solana blockchain. Unlike Ethereum's per-token contract model, a single shared program manages all SPL token operations, with individual mints and token accounts as data accounts. Token-2022 (2023) extends the base program with transfer hooks, confidential transfers, interest-bearing logic, and metadata extensions.
owner: Solana Foundation / solana-program organization
stack: Associated Token Account program for canonical per-wallet token accounts

entityAttributes.permissions: Permissionless token creation (anyone may create a mint); optional freeze authority; optional mint authority; Token-2022 adds configurable transfer restrictions and compliance extensions
entityAttributes.developmentTools: spl-token CLI; @solana/spl-token (JavaScript/TypeScript); Anchor framework; Metaplex Token Metadata program (for NFT metadata); Solana Playground; Hardhat-equivalent: Anchor testing suite
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Production standard; all major Solana wallets and DEXes support SPL tokens; Token-2022 reaching maturity with growing adoption for enterprise and regulated asset use cases
entityAttributes.trustFramework: Trustless: program is immutable on-chain; mint and freeze authorities controlled by configurable keypairs or multisig; Token-2022 confidential transfers use zero-knowledge proofs; trust model governed by Solana consensus
entityAttributes.targetApplicationDomain: Fungible tokens (stablecoins, governance, utility); NFTs; DeFi protocols; gaming assets; civic tokens and participation credentials; regulated asset issuance (Token-2022)
entityAttributes.deploymentModel: Singleton shared program; token mints and user token accounts are separate data accounts; no per-token contract deployment

name: Anchor Framework
description: The dominant development framework for Solana smart contracts ('programs'), providing a Rust eDSL with macros, IDL generation, type-safe client generation, and built-in security checks (account validation, ownership verification, signer constraints). Plays the role on Solana that Hardhat/Truffle play on Ethereum, with the additional responsibility of defining the standard patterns by which Solana programs are structured, serialized, and called.
owner: Originally: coral-xyz (Armani Ferrante / Anchor community); Stewardship transferred to Solana Foundation (solana-foundation/anchor)
stack: emits IDL from which TypeScript/JavaScript clients are auto-generated

entityAttributes.permissions: Open-source framework; permissionless program deployment to Solana mainnet; programs may be marked as upgradeable or immutable at deployment
entityAttributes.developmentTools: anchor-cli; Anchor Version Manager (AVM); @coral-xyz/anchor (TypeScript client library); Anchor Playground; VSCode Rust plugins; Solana Test Validator
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: De facto standard for Solana development; the large majority of new Solana programs use Anchor; IDL format is widely supported by wallets, explorers, and tooling; active development with regular releases
entityAttributes.trustFramework: Security enforced via compile-time macro constraints (account ownership, signer checks, account discriminators); Anchor reduces common Solana security pitfalls; deployed program trust model is Solana consensus
entityAttributes.targetApplicationDomain: All Solana smart contract development: DeFi, NFT platforms, DAOs, gaming, token programs, civic applications; serves as the programming interface layer for most Solana protocols
entityAttributes.deploymentModel: Framework for program development; programs deployed as upgradeable or immutable BPF bytecode accounts on Solana; each program has a unique program ID

name: XCM
description: Cross-Consensus Messaging Format: a universal, versioned message format for expressing instructions between heterogeneous consensus systems (parachains, relay chains, smart contracts, bridges). XCM is a messaging format, not a protocol — it defines what messages mean, not how they are transported. Built around a XCVM (Cross-Consensus Virtual Machine) with a register-based instruction set. Enables trustless asset transfers, remote execution, and cross-chain governance across the Polkadot ecosystem and beyond.
owner: Polkadot Fellows (polkadot-fellows/xcm-format); originally authored by Gavin Wood / Parity Technologies
stack: XCM instructions compile to XCVM bytecode

entityAttributes.permissions: Permissionless message sending for whitelisted origins; chain-level governance controls which cross-chain operations are permitted; XCM itself is chain-agnostic
entityAttributes.developmentTools: Polkadot SDK (formerly Substrate); xcm-simulator for testing; XCM Playground; Chopsticks (local fork testing); polkadot-js API; XCM Executor and Builder libraries
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Production standard across 40+ Polkadot parachains; v4 is the current canonical version; IBC expansion (IBC Eureka) and growing non-Polkadot adoption; more relevant to Polkadot ecosystem than ink! following Polkadot's EVM pivot
entityAttributes.trustFramework: Trust-minimized: message validity enforced by Polkadot shared security (relay chain); XCM execution at destination chain is cryptographically verified; no relayer trust required for XCMP; HRMP channels require governance approval
entityAttributes.targetApplicationDomain: Cross-parachain DeFi; cross-chain governance and voting; interchain asset transfers; cross-chain NFT operations; sovereign staking; multi-chain DAO coordination; bridge communication
entityAttributes.deploymentModel: Integrated as a Substrate pallet in each parachain runtime; XCM version negotiation via channel handshake; transport layer (XCMP/HRMP) is separate from the format itself

name: FA2 (TZIP-12)
description: Financial Application 2 (TZIP-12): Tezos' unified multi-asset token interface standard supporting fungible tokens, NFTs, and semi-fungible tokens within a single contract. Defines a flexible, permission-configurable token interface with configurable transfer policies (monolith, transfer hook, or wrapper patterns). Equivalent in scope to ERC-20 + ERC-721 + ERC-1155 combined. The dominant token standard for the Tezos ecosystem.
owner: Tezos ecosystem / TQ Tezos (original proposal); maintained by Tezos Foundation and community

entityAttributes.permissions: Configurable permission policies: self-transfer, operator-approved transfer, no-transfer; operator model for delegated transfers; mint/burn entry points not standardized (implementer discretion)
entityAttributes.developmentTools: SmartPy IDE; LIGO; Taquito (JavaScript client library); Tezos CLI (octez-client); Temple Wallet; Better Call Dev (contract explorer)
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Final TZIP; dominant Tezos token standard adopted by all major wallets, DEXes, and NFT platforms on Tezos (objkt.com, fx(hash), Versum); FA2.1 draft extends the standard
entityAttributes.trustFramework: Trustless: Michelson contract logic enforces transfer permissions; on-chain operator registry; no admin key in base standard; trust model governed by Tezos liquid proof-of-stake consensus and Michelson formal verification tools
entityAttributes.targetApplicationDomain: Fungible tokens; NFTs and digital art (major Tezos NFT ecosystem); semi-fungible tokens; regulated asset tokenization (DAR-1 standard built on FA2); civic and social tokens; DAO governance tokens
entityAttributes.deploymentModel: Per-project contract origination on Tezos; single contract supports all token types and multiple token IDs; optional multi-asset architecture

name: FileCoin
description: Decentralized storage network with marketplace for storage and retrieval services using blockchain incentives
owner: Protocol Labs (Juan Benet, Founder/CEO)

entityAttributes.p2pArchitecture: DHT-based decentralized storage network with proof-of-storage consensus
entityAttributes.overlayNetwork: libp2p with Kademlia DHT
entityAttributes.contentAddressing: Yes (CID via IPFS content addressing)
entityAttributes.local-first: No (network storage protocol, not local-first)
entityAttributes.e2ee: N/A (client-side encryption optional; not protocol-level E2EE)
entityAttributes.byzantineFaultTolerance: Yes (consensus via Proof-of-Replication, Proof-of-Spacetime)
entityAttributes.signature: Cryptographic proofs (PoRep, PoSt) + BLS signatures
entityAttributes.permissions: Market-based access control, smart contract permissions via FVM
entityAttributes.semanticWebCompatability: N/A (can store any content including RDF, but no native semantic web support)
entityAttributes.smartContract: Yes (FVM - Filecoin Virtual Machine, launched March 14, 2023)
entityAttributes.assetValueEmbedding: Native (FIL token is the protocol's sole medium of exchange; storage deal collateral, miner block rewards, retrieval payments, and on-chain penalties all denominated and settled in FIL; no off-chain settlement path at base layer)
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Proprietary Open Standard (FIPs — Filecoin Improvement Proposals; detailed protocol specification; built on IPFS/IPLD standards)
entityAttributes.updatePolicy: Immutable / Append-only (content-addressed CIDs are immutable by definition; updating data requires storing a new CID; mutable references possible via IPNS or FVM smart contract pointers but not native to base storage protocol)
entityAttributes.storageProofMechanism: Hybrid PoRep + PoSt: Proof of Replication (PoRep) cryptographically proves a miner physically sealed a unique copy of client data at deal start; Proof of Spacetime (PoSt) — WindowPoSt and WinningPoSt — continuously proves data is retained over time; both verified on-chain via zkSNARK circuits
entityAttributes.economicIncentiveModel: Token rewards + market fees (FIL native token): storage providers earn FIL block rewards (WinningPoSt) plus client deal fees; retrieval providers earn FIL via off-chain payment channels; providers stake FIL collateral slashed on PoSt failure; deal pricing set by open market between clients and providers
entityAttributes.retrievalGuarantee: Storage: cryptoeconomically enforced (PoSt failures trigger collateral slashing); Retrieval: best-effort (retrieval market currently lacks on-chain enforcement; providers are paid via off-chain micropayment channels; Saturn CDN and future Retrieval Markets aim to strengthen guarantees)

name: Arweave
description: Permanent, pay-once decentralized storage network built on a blockweave data structure, where miners are incentivized through Succinct Proofs of Random Access (SPoRA) to store rare data perpetually. Hosts the 'permaweb' — an immutable, censorship-resistant web of permanently stored applications and data.
owner: Minimum Spanning Technologies Ltd. / Sam Williams (CEO)

entityAttributes.p2pArchitecture: Public blockchain (blockweave with SPoRA consensus)
entityAttributes.overlayNetwork: Global
entityAttributes.contentAddressing: Yes (transaction IDs are SHA-256 content hashes)
entityAttributes.local-first: N/A (miners replicate; clients are ephemeral)
entityAttributes.e2ee: No (data is publicly readable by default; encryption is opt-in at app layer)
entityAttributes.byzantineFaultTolerance: Yes (SPoRA consensus; democratic content policy for node-level moderation)
entityAttributes.signature: RSA-4096 (wallet signatures); SHA-256 content hashing
entityAttributes.permissions: N/A (fully public by default; access control at application layer)
entityAttributes.smartContract: Yes (via SmartWeave / Warp contracts on-chain)
entityAttributes.assetValueEmbedding: Native (AR token embedded at transaction/block level; storage fees paid in AR)
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: De Facto Standard (stable, widely adopted permaweb; no formal standards body)
entityAttributes.updatePolicy: Immutable / Append-only (data written to blockweave cannot be deleted or modified; IPNS-like mutable pointers available via SmartWeave)
entityAttributes.storageProofMechanism: Proof of Access (PoA) / Succinct Proofs of Random Access (SPoRA) — miners must prove access to a randomly selected historical block to mine new blocks, incentivizing wide data replication
entityAttributes.economicIncentiveModel: One-time endowment (permanent storage model): upfront AR token fee funds a storage endowment; interest from endowment pays miners indefinitely as storage costs decline
entityAttributes.retrievalGuarantee: Best-effort (incentivized but not formally guaranteed; data persistence depends on endowment viability and miner participation)

name: Storj
description: S3-compatible decentralized cloud object storage platform that splits client-side encrypted files into 80 erasure-coded shards distributed across thousands of independent storage nodes globally. Provides enterprise-grade durability (11 nines) at significantly lower cost than centralized providers.
owner: Storj Labs, Inc.

entityAttributes.p2pArchitecture: DHT (nodes discovered via satellite; direct client-to-node uploads)
entityAttributes.overlayNetwork: Global
entityAttributes.contentAddressing: Yes (erasure-coded shards addressed by hash; file addressed by metadata on satellite)
entityAttributes.local-first: N/A (cloud storage model)
entityAttributes.e2ee: Yes (client-side encryption before upload; zero-knowledge architecture — Storj nodes see only encrypted shards)
entityAttributes.byzantineFaultTolerance: Partial (erasure coding tolerates node failures; Satellite is semi-centralized coordination layer)
entityAttributes.signature: ECDSA (macaroon-based access grants); TLS for node-satellite comms
entityAttributes.permissions: Cryptographic Capabilities (macaroon/ZKAP access grants with caveats; UCANs-like delegation)
entityAttributes.smartContract: No
entityAttributes.assetValueEmbedding: Application-layer add-on (STORJ ERC-20 token used for payment; not embedded at protocol level)
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: De Facto Standard (proprietary open standard; widely deployed production system)
entityAttributes.updatePolicy: Mutable (latest only) — objects can be overwritten or deleted; versioning available via S3-compatible APIs
entityAttributes.storageProofMechanism: Erasure coding with audit (Satellite continuously audits nodes by requesting random shards; failed audits reduce node reputation and payment)
entityAttributes.economicIncentiveModel: Fiat/stablecoin payments (node operators paid in STORJ tokens or USD based on storage + bandwidth provided; Satellite manages billing)
entityAttributes.retrievalGuarantee: Strong (SLA-backed; 11-nines durability; 80-shard erasure coding means any 29 of 80 shards sufficient for retrieval)

name: Sia / Skynet
description: Sia is a decentralized cloud storage network where renters form cryptographic storage contracts with hosts on a Proof-of-Work blockchain, paying in Siacoin (SC). Skynet (now defunct as a hosted service, Sia continues) was a layer-2 content delivery and decentralized web protocol built on top of Sia, enabling file sharing via persistent Skylinks.
owner: Sia Foundation (nonprofit, Luke Champine) — core Sia protocol; Skynet Labs (David Vorick) — defunct since Nov 2022

entityAttributes.p2pArchitecture: Public blockchain (PoW; host-renter contracts via blockchain; Skynet adds portal layer)
entityAttributes.overlayNetwork: Global
entityAttributes.contentAddressing: Yes (file shards addressed by Merkle hash; Skylinks encode content-addressed metadata)
entityAttributes.local-first: N/A (cloud storage model)
entityAttributes.e2ee: Yes (client-side encryption of uploaded files before storage on hosts)
entityAttributes.byzantineFaultTolerance: Partial (PoW blockchain is BFT; individual host honesty enforced by cryptographic storage contracts and collateral slashing)
entityAttributes.permissions: Cryptographic Capabilities (storage contracts with host collateral; Skynet registry uses public key access control)
entityAttributes.smartContract: Yes (storage contracts are on-chain smart contracts in Siacoin blockchain)
entityAttributes.assetValueEmbedding: Native (Siacoin SC embedded at blockchain level; storage contracts settle in SC)
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: De Facto Standard (established since 2015; community standard for decentralized storage; no formal standards body)
entityAttributes.updatePolicy: Mutable (versioned) for Sia files (hosts can update via new contracts); Skynet Registry supports mutable entries; immutable content-addressed files via Skylinks
entityAttributes.storageProofMechanism: Merkle audit proof (hosts must submit cryptographic storage proofs on-chain at contract end; failure results in collateral burn and missed payment)
entityAttributes.economicIncentiveModel: Token rewards (native token SC): renters lock SC in contracts; hosts earn SC for proven storage; both parties lose collateral for breach
entityAttributes.retrievalGuarantee: Best-effort (incentivized via cryptographic contracts and collateral; retrieval availability depends on host uptime and contract renewal)

name: Swarm
description: Decentralized storage and communication platform built on Ethereum, designed as the 'hard disk of the world computer.' Files are split into 4KB chunks, content-addressed, and distributed across a P2P network of Bee nodes, with storage incentivized via the BZZ token and a postage stamp economic model.
owner: Swarm Foundation (nonprofit)

entityAttributes.p2pArchitecture: DHT (Kademlia-based chunk routing via libp2p)
entityAttributes.overlayNetwork: Global
entityAttributes.contentAddressing: Yes (chunk content hashes; Single-Owner Chunks use owner-signed addresses)
entityAttributes.local-first: N/A (cloud/P2P model; nodes cache popular chunks)
entityAttributes.e2ee: Yes (Trojan chunks enable covert encrypted messaging; Single-Owner Chunks provide zero-leak privacy)
entityAttributes.byzantineFaultTolerance: Partial (Kademlia routing is resilient; economic incentives deter malicious behavior; no formal BFT consensus)
entityAttributes.signature: ECDSA (Ethereum-compatible; BZZ cheque signing)
entityAttributes.permissions: Cryptographic Capabilities (Single-Owner Chunks with owner-signed access; postage stamps as access tokens)
entityAttributes.smartContract: Yes (postage stamp contract and chequebook contract on Gnosis Chain)
entityAttributes.protocolStackPosition: Application-layer (built on TCP/IP; Ethereum smart contracts for settlement)
entityAttributes.assetValueEmbedding: Native (BZZ token embedded in protocol; postage stamps = pre-paid storage access; SWAP accounting built into node-to-node bandwidth exchange)
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Community Standard (actively deployed; no formal standards body)
entityAttributes.updatePolicy: Mutable (versioned) via Swarm Feeds (mutable documents with owner-controlled update stream); immutable by default for content-addressed chunks
entityAttributes.storageProofMechanism: None formal at chunk level (postage stamp expiry model: stamps decrease in value over time; expired chunks evicted; redistribution mechanism rewards nodes holding valid stamped chunks via stake-weighted lottery)
entityAttributes.economicIncentiveModel: Token rewards (BZZ): publishers purchase postage stamps; BZZ redistributed to storage node operators via stake-weighted redistribution; bandwidth compensated via SWAP (off-chain cheque) system
entityAttributes.retrievalGuarantee: Best-effort (incentivized but not cryptoeconomically enforced at individual chunk level; popular content well-cached; cold data may be lost after stamp expiry)

name: Tahoe-LAFS
description: Free and open-source decentralized file store built on a 'principle of least authority' (POLA) security model, providing provider-independent security: files are client-encrypted and erasure-coded (default 3-of-10) before distribution across storage servers, such that no server operator can read or modify user data.
owner: Tahoe-LAFS Software Foundation (volunteer-maintained open source project); originally started at Allmydata.com

entityAttributes.p2pArchitecture: Mesh network (client connects to introducer, then to storage nodes; no routing protocol between nodes)
entityAttributes.overlayNetwork: Store-wide (data limited to configured storage grid)
entityAttributes.contentAddressing: Yes (immutable files addressed by content hash embedded in read capability)
entityAttributes.local-first: N/A (client-server model; gateway on local machine or remote)
entityAttributes.e2ee: Yes (client-side AES encryption before upload; storage servers never see plaintext)
entityAttributes.byzantineFaultTolerance: Partial (erasure coding tolerates node failure; cryptographic proofs prevent silent data corruption; no Byzantine consensus protocol)
entityAttributes.signature: Ed25519 (mutable file signatures)
entityAttributes.permissions: Cryptographic Capabilities (read caps, write caps, verify caps — capability-based access control; no ACLs)
entityAttributes.smartContract: No
entityAttributes.assetValueEmbedding: None (no token; volunteer or self-hosted model)
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: De Facto Standard (established open-source reference for capability-based decentralized storage; 15+ year history; no formal standards body)
entityAttributes.updatePolicy: Immutable (for immutable files) / Mutable (versioned) for mutable directories and files (MDMF format)
entityAttributes.storageProofMechanism: Merkle audit proof (continuous integrity checks; storage servers audited by requesting random shares; failed audits trigger data repair)
entityAttributes.economicIncentiveModel: Volunteer (no economic incentive model; operators run nodes for altruistic or cooperative reasons; lease-based GC to manage storage)
entityAttributes.retrievalGuarantee: Best-effort (configurable K-of-N erasure coding; 3-of-10 default tolerates 7 node failures; availability depends on operator commitments)

name: Züs
description: High-performance enterprise-grade decentralized cloud storage platform (formerly 0Chain) with a proprietary two-layer blockchain architecture separating consensus (Miners/Sharders) from storage (Blobbers). Features three-layer data security (fragmentation, proxy re-encryption, erasure coding), QoS-enforced storage agreements, and a ZCN token economy.
owner: Züs (formerly 0Chain); Saswata Basu (CEO), Prof. Tom Austin

entityAttributes.p2pArchitecture: Public Blockchain (DPoS-based two-layer chain; storage on Blobber sidechain)
entityAttributes.overlayNetwork: Global
entityAttributes.contentAddressing: Yes (files addressed by Merkle hash across Blobbers)
entityAttributes.local-first: N/A (cloud model)
entityAttributes.e2ee: Yes (proxy re-encryption for private sharing; data encrypted client-side)
entityAttributes.byzantineFaultTolerance: Yes (squared-staking DPoS consensus; Sybil resistance via randomized view change; Blobber slashing for non-performance)
entityAttributes.permissions: Cryptographic Capabilities (proxy re-encryption keys for sharing; write markers as cryptographic commitments)
entityAttributes.smartContract: Yes (storage smart contracts on Züs blockchain; ZüsDAO governance)
entityAttributes.assetValueEmbedding: Native (ZCN token embedded in storage contracts; 'Store = Stake' model links storage to locked ZCN)
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Community Standard (production deployed; proprietary open standard; no formal standards body)
entityAttributes.updatePolicy: Mutable (versioned) — files can be updated via new write markers; immutability available as configuration option
entityAttributes.storageProofMechanism: Proof of Access (PoA challenges) — Miners issue random challenges to Blobbers; Blobbers must return correct data proofs; failure results in ZCN slashing from challenge pool
entityAttributes.economicIncentiveModel: Token rewards (ZCN native token): clients lock ZCN for storage; Blobbers stake ZCN as collateral; reward pool distributed to Blobbers for proven storage; 'Store = Stake' model ties storage capacity to locked ZCN
entityAttributes.retrievalGuarantee: Strong (QoS protocol enforces performance SLAs; cryptoeconomic slashing for non-performance; near wire-speed retrieval design goal)

name: Codex
description: Decentralized Durability Engine (DDE) and P2P storage protocol developed under the Logos technology stack (IFT/Status), designed for censorship-resistant, durable data storage using erasure coding, ZK-proof-based remote auditing, and a marketplace for incentivizing storage providers. Targets archival and cold storage use cases with 'lazy' data repair.
owner: IFT (formerly Logos/Status); Dmitriy Ryajov (CTO)

entityAttributes.p2pArchitecture: DHT (Kademlia-based via nim-libp2p; block exchange protocol similar to BitSwap)
entityAttributes.overlayNetwork: Global
entityAttributes.contentAddressing: Yes (IPFS-compatible CIDs)
entityAttributes.local-first: N/A (distributed storage)
entityAttributes.e2ee: Yes (planned; privacy-preserving design goal of Logos stack)
entityAttributes.byzantineFaultTolerance: Planned (ZK proof-based remote auditing for storage verification; marketplace with slashing)
entityAttributes.signature: Ed25519 / ECDSA (libp2p standard); ZK-SNARKs (Poseidon2 hash for storage proofs)
entityAttributes.permissions: N/A (currently open; marketplace-based permissions planned)
entityAttributes.smartContract: Planned for future (Codex Marketplace contracts for storage agreements and payments)
entityAttributes.assetValueEmbedding: Application-layer add-on (CDX token planned for marketplace; not yet deployed)
entityAttributes.protocolMaturityStandardization: Pre-specification / Academic-Research (whitepaper published; testnet live; mainnet not yet launched; no standards body involvement)
entityAttributes.updatePolicy: Immutable / Append-only (content-addressed blocks are immutable; appendable data planned as future feature)

entityAttributes.economicIncentiveModel: Token rewards (CDX token planned): storage providers stake CDX as collateral; clients pay CDX for storage marketplace slots; validators earn CDX for processing challenges
entityAttributes.retrievalGuarantee: Best-effort (incentivized via marketplace contracts; cryptographic proofs verify storage; lazy repair mechanism restores lost shards; mainnet guarantees not yet established)
